qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via unvalidated URL fetch in Node.js image processing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a Local File Inclusion/Path Traversal vulnerability in jsPDF (GHSA-f8cm-6447-x5h2) that survey-pdf inherits as a dependency. This finding instead reports an SSRF in survey-pdf's own ImageUtils._getImageInfo() method in src/utils/image/node.ts, which calls fetch() with user-controlled URLs. The vulnerable code and root cause are different: the advisory root cause is jsPDF's path traversal/LFI in its image/file handling code, while this finding's root cause is survey-pdf's own unvalidated fetch() call. They are separate vulnerabilities in separate components.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory (GHSA-h3q6-jfrg-3x6q) describes a Local File Inclusion/Path Traversal vulnerability in jsPDF <= 3.0.4 (GHSA-f8cm-6447-x5h2) that survey-pdf inherits through its dependency. The sole qualifying finding reports an SSRF in survey-pdf's own fetch() call in src/utils/image/node.ts, which is a different vulnerability in a different component (survey-pdf's own code vs. jsPDF's path traversal). The advisory root cause is jsPDF's LFI/path traversal, not survey-pdf's unvalidated URL fetch.
evidence: Advisory references jsPDF vulnerability GHSA-f8cm-6447-x5h2 (LFI/Path Traversal) with CWE-35 and CWE-73
Advisory fix is upgrading jsPDF dependency to >= 4.0.0, confirming root cause is in jsPDF
SSRF finding's vulnerable code is src/utils/image/node.ts:11 in survey-pdf's own ImageUtils, not in jsPDF
